title: Thin Scope, Vertical Slices, and Evidence-Driven Roadmap · Mastery Review
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for thin scope, vertical slices, and evidence-driven roadmap and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for risk-ordered vertical-slice roadmap. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.
Slice work across interface, behavior, data, verification, accessibility, security, and operations to produce one end-to-end learning signal.
Order slices by prerequisite knowledge, risk retirement, feedback value, and reversible commitment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Story Outcome · changed case · Quiz Story Outcome
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

Which action demonstrates Story Outcome without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Story Outcome · changed case · Quiz Story Outcome.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence. For Story Outcome · changed case · Quiz Story Outcome, keep Story Outcome connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Write a bounded user scenario with trigger, actor, need, outcome, constraints, and observable acceptance evidence.

content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Story Outcome result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub profile and project presentation documentation — Controls selective project presentation, useful README evidence, setup, demonstration, testing, dependency currency, and truthful portfolio communication.

steps.instruction:

stimulus.title: Scope Tradeoff failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Scope Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Scope Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A six-week backlog completes database, API, and interface layers separately while no affected user can finish one end-to-end task. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Scope Tradeoff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Scope Tradeoff, keep Scope Tradeoff connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Protect the outcome by trading feature breadth, fidelity, automation, and polish while preserving non-negotiable quality boundaries.
